issuesFound.title: Theme toggle missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher uses a delegated click listener on elements with the data-theme-toggle attribute but does not ensure these elements are native buttons or have keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate the theme toggle if it is applied to a non-interactive element like a div or span.

issuesFound.title: Comparison toggle missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module manages the comparison table visibility but does not initialise the aria-expanded state on the trigger element [data-expand-comparison] during the init phase. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the collapsible content.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panel role mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard assigns role="tabpanel" to step containers but does not provide a corresponding tablist or tab elements. This violates the ARIA tab pattern requirements and confuses assistive technology users who expect a tabbed interface.

issuesFound.title: Carousel dot role mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel navigation dots are given role="tab", but the slides they control are not given role="tabpanel". This creates a broken ARIA relationship where tabs exist without associated panels.

issuesFound.title: Filter chips update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter chips container is updated dynamically when filters are added or removed, but the container is not a live region. Users of assistive technology are not notified when a filter is removed via a chip interaction.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code is validated, the status message is updated, but the aria-invalid attribute is never set on the input field itself. Assistive technologies will not communicate that the field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter missing live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The stat counter animates numbers from zero to a target value, but it only updates the aria-label once the animation is complete. Screen reader users may miss the final value if they are not focused on the element at the exact moment the animation ends.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions missing group label association
issuesFound.explanation: The "Recent searches" group label is inserted into the listbox as a presentation element with aria-hidden="true". This prevents screen reader users from understanding the categorization of the suggestions.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1590: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute on the field. Assistive technologies will not identify the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1485: `function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }`

issuesFound.title: click handler on non-native element without matching keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher uses a delegated click listener on elements that may not be native buttons. Without a keydown handler for Enter and Space, keyboard-only users cannot change the site theme.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4345: `document.addEventListener('click', function _onThemeClick(ev) { ... }); document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKey(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === 'Enter' || ev.key === ' ') { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause or stop control
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications are automatically dismissed via setTimeout without providing the user a mechanism to adjust or disable the timing. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: `let timerId = null; if (dur > 0 && !CONFIG.accessibility.disableToastAutohide) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); } toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId));`

issuesFound.title: incorrect ARIA role for assertive live region
issuesFound.explanation: The assertive live region is initialized with role="status", which has an implicit politeness of "polite". Assertive announcements should use role="alert" to ensure they interrupt current speech.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: `const wrapper = createElement('div', { role: politeness === 'assertive' ? 'alert' : 'status', 'aria-live': politeness, 'aria-atomic': 'true', 'aria-relevant': 'additions text', id: `luminary-live-${politeness}`, });`

issuesFound.title: invalid ARIA attribute on table header
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table highlighter applies aria-selected to <th> elements. The aria-selected attribute is not supported on table headers and provides no semantic value to screen readers in this context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: `qsa('[data-plan-head]', _tableEl).forEach(th => { const isTarget = th.getAttribute('data-plan-head') === planId; toggleClass(th, CSS.featured, isTarget); });`

issuesFound.title: progress bar missing required ARIA roles and attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard uses a div as a progress bar but fails to ensure it has the required role="progressbar" and value attributes (aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax) during initialization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3965: `_progressBar = qs('[data-wizard-progress]', _form); if (_progressBar) { _progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100'); }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.fix: Line 250: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none with no alternative visual differentiation (like underline on hover). Users with colour blindness or low vision cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 268: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes all focus outlines for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance. Keyboard-only and screen reader users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 272: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 400: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This does not hide content visually or from screen readers; it makes it visible. Screen reader users will hear duplicated content, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transition with transform and box-shadow, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation, which can cause discomfort for those with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 664: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card selector has a transition with transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only sets transform: none on hover. The transition property itself is not disabled, so motion may still occur. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a:hover::after
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:hover::after selector has a transform transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only sets transform: none. The transition property is not disabled, so motion may still occur. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a:hover::after { transform: none; } .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Toggle function for billing period does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and does not supply any alternative visual cue, so button focus becomes invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn includes a transition on transform, causing spatial movement. No @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion) rule disables this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-ctas . btn-primary defines a transition that animates transform on hover, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for users who disable animations.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform change)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies a translateY transform, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

Increase the minimum height, e.g.:

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height: 20px)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-lg (min-height: 36px)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min-width/height: 20px)

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.fix:
Use a non‑negative value, e.g.:

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on a { text-decoration: none; }
issuesFound.explanation: Global removal of underlines from links eliminates a primary visual cue that distinguishes links from surrounding text, and no alternative styling (border, background, weight) is provided.
issuesFound.fix:
Restore underlines or add another visual differentiator, e.g.:

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a transition on `transform`, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, and no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override is provided for this selector.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override, e.g.:  
  Line 240: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` transitions `transform`, producing motion that lacks a reduced‑motion guard, leaving users who prefer reduced motion without a fallback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override, e.g.:  
  Line 460: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Carousel play button missing aria‑pressed initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button for the testimonial carousel receives aria‑pressed only after the first user interaction. When the page loads the button has no aria‑pressed state, violating the requirement that toggle buttons expose their state to AT from the start.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button that opens the mobile navigation receives aria‑expanded and aria‑controls but no visible label or aria‑label. If the button contains only an icon, screen‑reader users have no name for the control.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “back‑to‑top” control is a button that contains only an icon. The script sets aria‑hidden but never supplies an aria‑label or visible text, leaving the button nameless for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide a label when the button is created or in the markup, e.g.:  

```js
setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label',
  STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');
```

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code field missing label

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons are identified only by data attributes; they have no visible text and no `aria-label`, leaving them invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add descriptive `aria-label`s (or visible text). Example HTML change:  

```html
<button data-carousel-prev aria-label="Previous testimonial">←</button>
<button data-carousel-next aria-label="Next testimonial">→</button>
```  

If the buttons are generated, set the attributes in JS after selection:  

```js
if (_prevBtn) _prevBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Previous testimonial');
if (_nextBtn) _nextBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Next testimonial');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button missing initial accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button receives an `aria-label` only after the first click (`_onToggleClick`). Until then it has no accessible name, which is a barrier for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button may lack `aria-controls` when the drawer has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: If the navigation drawer element does not have an `id`, the toggle button never receives an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship required for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard progress bar missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar has `role="progressbar"` and numeric ARIA values but no `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` to convey what the progress represents. Screen‑reader users receive only the numeric value without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide a label. Example HTML change: ```html
``` If the element is generated, set in JS after selection: ```js if (_progressBar && !_progressBar.getAttribute('aria-label')) { _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Form completion progress'); } ```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance` the panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `true`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot tell whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance` after the panel is made visible.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register` the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` is set. Assistive technologies may assume the dropdown is expanded on load.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating the trigger, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open() does not set aria‑expanded to true on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open` adds CSS classes and shows the drawer, but never updates the hamburger button’s `aria-expanded` to `true`. Users of assistive technology cannot detect the navigation state.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `mobileNav.open`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the field. Validation errors are therefore invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In `srUtils.markInvalid` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on fields that become valid
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid` removes error styling and describedby but does not remove `aria-invalid`. Once a field is corrected, the invalid state persists for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `srUtils.markValid` add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not remove aria‑describedby when an error is cleared
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid` clears the error message text but leaves the `aria-describedby` reference to the now‑removed error element, causing a dangling reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Extend `srUtils.markValid` to also remove the error element’s ID from the field’s `aria-describedby` list (or clear the attribute entirely if no other IDs remain).  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu closeInstance does not reset aria‑expanded on trigger when panel is hidden via outside click
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` sets `aria-expanded="false"` only when called directly, but the outside‑click handler (`_onOutsideClick`) calls `close()` without passing the trigger, leaving the trigger’s `aria-expanded` unchanged.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure `_onOutsideClick` calls `close()` which internally sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, or explicitly set it in the outside‑click handler.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion deep‑link handling does not update URL hash when a panel is opened via keyboard (Enter/Space)
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerKeydown` opens a panel on Enter/Space but only updates the hash inside `_openInstance` when `hashDeepLink` is true. However, `_openInstance` is not called; the code directly calls `open(item)` which does not modify the hash, so the URL does not reflect the opened FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix:
In the keyboard handler, after opening the item, call `history.replaceState(null, '', \`#${item.el.id}\`);` or ensure the open function updates the hash.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table highlighter does not set aria‑selected on column headers when a column is highlighted
issuesFound.explanation: `planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn` toggles CSS classes but only sets `aria-selected="true"` on the matching header; however, it does not remove `aria-selected` from previously highlighted headers, leaving multiple headers marked as selected.
issuesFound.fix:
In the loop that iterates over all `[data-plan-head]` elements, ensure `th.removeAttribute('aria-selected')` for non‑matching headers before setting it on the active one.  

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload field does not announce successful upload when `prefers‑reduced‑motion` is enabled
issuesFound.explanation: When `prefersReducedMotion` is true, `_uploadEntry` skips the animation but still calls `liveRegion.announce`, however the live region element may be hidden (`aria-live` not set) because the status element is never made visible.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element (`_statusEl`) is given `aria-live="polite"` and is not hidden when announcing upload success, even when reduced motion is preferred.

fpTitles: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on fields that become valid
Inline validator does not remove aria‑describedby when an error is cleared
Dropdown menu closeInstance does not reset aria‑expanded on trigger when panel is hidden via outside click
FAQ accordion deep‑link handling does not update URL hash when a panel is opened via keyboard (Enter/Space)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not set aria‑expanded to true when opening
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes and focus handling but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute to true, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation drawer is now open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside the open() function after the drawer is shown.  

---

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria‑expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are parsed in dropdownMenu.register() the code ensures aria‑haspopup but never initializes aria‑expanded (on the trigger) to false, violating the requirement that hidden widgets expose a defined collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
In register() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setting aria‑haspopup.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion parses each FAQ item and sets aria‑controls and role, but does not set aria‑expanded to false for collapsed items, leaving the accessible state undefined on load.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating trigger in faqRegistry.parse() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when item.open is false.  

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate() function marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so screen‑reader users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
In _inlineValidator._renderResult() when result.valid is false call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true'); (and remove it when valid).  

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator only removes visual error classes but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing persistent false error state for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
In _inlineValidator._renderResult() when result.valid is true call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');.  

---

issuesFound.title: dynamic content updates in productFilter.applyFilters() lack live‑region announcement for count changes on some browsers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the code updates the count badge but does not always update a dedicated aria‑live region, so users relying on screen readers may miss the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After _updateCountBadge() add liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} shown`); or reuse existing live region.  

---

fpTitles: inlineValidator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance opens a dropdown panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute to "true". Screen‑reader users are not informed that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded to true when opening
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open adds CSS classes and focus‑traps the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the hamburger button. Assistive technologies cannot detect the opened state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, after opening the drawer call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); (and ensure aria-controls is present).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error text but only calls srUtils.markInvalid, which adds aria‑describedby without setting aria‑invalid="true". The required ARIA state is missing, breaking accessibility for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when result.valid is false add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); (and remove it when the field becomes valid).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form status messages lack an ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The status element created in newsletterForm.init has no aria‑live attribute, so success or error messages are not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: When creating the status element, include 'aria-live': 'polite' (and optionally 'role': 'status') or set these attributes after creation.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button _selectors.searchClear is referenced but never given an aria‑label or visible label, making its purpose unclear to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: When selecting _clearBtn, add setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); or provide visible text.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger may miss aria‑controls when panel has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register, aria‑controls is set on the trigger only if the panel already has an id. If the panel lacks an id, the trigger has no aria‑controls, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every panel receives a generated id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel') ) before setting aria‑controls, or always set aria‑controls using the generated id.

fpTitles: Search clear button missing an accessible name
Dropdown trigger may miss aria‑controls when panel has no id

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded on the trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change panel visibility but never set aria‑expanded on the button that controls the menu, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed when acting as two‑state toggles
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger is a button that toggles between open and closed states, yet aria‑pressed is never updated, violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the menu state, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(isOpen)) or toggleAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', true/false).

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls linking to the navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas navigation does not reference the drawer via aria‑controls, preventing AT from locating the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: After locating _navDrawer, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _navDrawer.id || 'site-nav-drawer').

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are shown visually, but the aria‑invalid attribute is never added, so screen readers do not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true').

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: After a field is corrected, the visual error is cleared but aria‑invalid remains, leaving AT with stale state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when result.valid is true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid') or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The numeric count of visible products changes, but no aria‑live region is updated, so users of assistive technology are not informed of the new total.
issuesFound.fix: Create a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and update its textContent with the new count inside _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons do not expose their selected state with aria‑selected
issuesFound.explanation: Active filter tabs are indicated only by CSS classes; without aria‑selected AT cannot convey which tab is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _ syncTabAriaStates, after toggling the CSS class, also call setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)).

fpTitles: Inline form validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the aria‑expanded state on the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is created in the markup without an aria‑expanded attribute, so its initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show or hide a submenu but never toggle aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() expand or collapse an answer panel but never modify aria‑expanded on the question button, so the expanded state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is parsed, the trigger button receives aria‑controls but no aria‑expanded attribute, leaving its default state undefined for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() after creating trigger, initialise the attribute:
```js
if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error text but never sets aria‑invalid on the invalid field, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, the previous aria‑invalid state remains, causing persistent error indication for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the attribute is removed in the valid branch of _inlineValidator._renderResult() (as shown above).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle ( [data‑billing‑toggle] ) is given role="switch” and aria‑checked but no aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, so its purpose is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not initialise aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Although dropdownMenu.register() adds aria‑expanded="false" when missing, some triggers are created dynamically after init and never receive the attribute, leaving their state undefined.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating a trigger (in register()), ensure the attribute exists:
```js
if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed state

issuesFound.fix: In the open() and close() functions add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open)) and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false') respectively.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module’s open() and close() functions (lines 2104‑2116) change the visibility of FAQ panels but never modify the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger buttons act as toggles but the script does not set aria‑pressed to indicate the current state, which is required for button‑like toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After updating aria‑expanded, also call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑pressed', String(item.open)).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge() function (lines 1662‑1665) updates the visible count of products but does not send the change to an ARIA live region, so users of assistive technology are not informed when the result set changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region element (e.g., 
) and update its textContent inside _updateCountBadge() with a message such as “12 products found”.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate() function (lines 2368‑2374) calls srUtils.markInvalid() which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the field, so validation errors are not conveyed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() inside inlineValidator, after detecting an invalid result, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') and on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not cleared from aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same inline validator only adds aria‑invalid on error but never removes it when the field is corrected, leaving stale error state announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult() function, when result.valid is true, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid') to clear the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle widgets lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Both the mobile navigation drawer and the FAQ accordion are hidden on initial load, yet their trigger elements do not have aria‑expanded="false" set during initialization, violating the ARIA pattern for collapsible widgets.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init() and faqAccordion.init() functions, after locating the trigger elements, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') to establish the correct initial state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: `aria‑expanded` not initialised on page load for mobile‑nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the mobile navigation is hidden, but the trigger button (`_trigger`) never receives an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their collapsed state on load.

In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_trigger`:

  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add

issuesFound.title: `aria‑expanded` not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup="true"` but are never given an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, leaving their collapsed state undisclosed to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() adds event listeners to the hamburger button but never initializes aria‑expanded to false on page load, so assistive technologies cannot infer the default closed state.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining _trigger in init(), call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show/hide the dropdown panel but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _ openInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu.init() registers dropdowns but does not set aria‑expanded to false on each trigger at load, so screen readers assume the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In the registration loop, after creating inst, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, leaving the button’s state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (data‑billing‑toggle) changes the period but never toggles aria‑pressed on the button, violating the ARIA button‑state pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._onToggleClick() (and in the period‑button click handler) call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing initial aria‑pressed value
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.init() creates the toggle element but never sets an initial aria‑pressed state, so assistive tech cannot know which mode is active.
issuesFound.fix: After _toggleEl is obtained, set setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected with aria‑invalid (HIGH) – example newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid by adding error messages, but srUtils.markInvalid() only adds aria‑describedby and never sets aria‑invalid='true', so screen readers do not learn the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl) add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and in srUtils.markValid() remove or set aria-invalid='false'.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the numeric count badge but does not use a live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element (e.g., _statusEl) and after updating the badge call _statusEl.textContent = `${count} products shown`; or use liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not cleared aria‑invalid on valid input (MEDIUM) – example contact form “message” textarea
issuesFound.explanation: srUtils.markValid() removes aria‑describedby but does not clear aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In srUtils.markValid() add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing accessible name and ARIA state
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected as `SELECTORS.navToggle` (the hamburger button) is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer but never receives an accessible name (no text, no `aria-label`, no `title`) and its ARIA state (`aria‑expanded`, `aria‑controls`) is never set. Screen‑reader users cannot discover the purpose of the button or its current state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `aria‑hidden` and focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but it never receives `aria‑hidden` when closed nor is a focus trap applied. When the drawer is open, focus can escape to the underlying page, and when closed, screen readers may still read its content.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria‑label` (accessible name)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button has no visible text and no `aria‑label`/`title`, leaving it unnamed for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: (see Issue 1 – the same fix adds an `aria‑label`).

issuesFound.title: “Scroll‑to‑top” button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button identified by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is rendered with only an icon (or no text) and no `aria‑label`. Users of screen readers cannot discover its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons lack `aria‑pressed` and `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch billing periods (`data-period` attributes) are styled as a toggle group but never receive `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑expanded`) to convey the selected state to AT. The current period is only reflected visually.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing `aria‑controls` linking to the price region
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons do not indicate which region they control, so screen readers cannot associate the button with the price display that changes when the button is activated.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price numbers are updated in the DOM, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new price to screen‑reader users. This violates WCAG 2.2 §4.1.3.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded or collapsed, the script updates ARIA attributes (`aria-expanded`, `aria-hidden`) but does not send an announcement via a live region. Users of assistive technology are left unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input lacks an accessible name (`aria-label` or associated `<label>`)
issuesFound.explanation: The search field (`SELECTORS.searchInput`) is referenced by scripts but has no visible label and no `aria-label`. Screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search clear button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (`SELECTORS.searchClear`) is an icon‑only button with no text and no `aria-label`, making it invisible to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle (simple on/off switch) missing `aria-pressed` state update
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` is treated as a switch but only toggles a CSS class; it never updates `aria-pressed`, so AT cannot know its state.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 5 – add `aria-pressed` handling in `_onToggleClick` and `_syncToggleState`).

issuesFound.title: Clickable backdrop lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop">` is used as a clickable element to close the mobile navigation but it is a non‑interactive `<div>` with an `onClick` handler and no `role="button"`, `tabIndex`, or keyboard handler. Keyboard‑only users cannot activate it, and screen readers do not announce it as a control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay clickable without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div className="modal-overlay">` serves as a click target to dismiss the modal but is a non‑interactive `<div>` with an `onClick` handler and no `role="button"`, `tabIndex`, or keyboard support. This makes the dismiss action inaccessible to keyboard users and invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Newsletter form)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `<Button>` in `NewsletterForm` receives a `loading` prop (`isSubmitting`) but the component’s `isLoading` prop is never set, so `aria-busy` is not applied. Assistive technologies are not informed that the button is in a loading state, which can cause confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Contact form)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit `<Button>` in `ContactForm` uses a `loading` prop (`isSubmitting`) but the `Button` component expects `isLoading`. Consequently `aria-busy` is not set, leaving screen readers unaware of the loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on newsletter email input

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` but no element with id `newsletter-email-error` exists, breaking the relationship and providing no accessible description of the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` → `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email' : undefined}` and add `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email" role="alert">{email.state.error}</ErrorMessage>` after the input.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact name input
issuesFound.explanation: The `TextInput` for “Full name” sets `aria-describedby` to `contact-name-error`, but no element with that id is rendered, so error information is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}` → `aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name' : undefined}` and render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-name" role="alert">{name.state.error}</ErrorMessage>` after the input.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact email input
issuesFound.explanation: The `TextInput` for “Email address” references `contact-email-error` via `aria-describedby`, but no element with that id exists, preventing screen readers from hearing the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined}` → `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email' : undefined}` and add `<ErrorMessage id="contact-email" role="alert">{email.state.error}</ErrorMessage>` after the input.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact subject input
issuesFound.explanation: The `TextInput` for “Subject” uses `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"` without a matching element, breaking the error‑description link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: `aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined}` → `aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject' : undefined}` and render `<ErrorMessage id="contact-subject" role="alert">{subject.state.error}</ErrorMessage>` after the input.

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email includes an aria-describedby attribute that points to an element with id “newsletter-email-error”, but no such element exists in the markup. Screen reader users will not be informed of the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1005: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby (contact name)
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for “Full name” references an aria-describedby id “contact-name-error” that is never rendered, leaving the error message inaccessible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby (contact subject)
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for “Subject” references a non‑existent “contact-subject-error” via aria-describedby, preventing screen readers from hearing validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby (contact message)
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea for “Message” builds an aria-describedby list that includes “contact-message-error”, but no element with that id is rendered, so error feedback is lost to AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: aria-describedby={[ counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : '' ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} → aria-describedby={[ counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message' : '' ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy (newsletter submit)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit Button in NewsletterForm uses a custom prop “loading” instead of the defined “isLoading”. Consequently, the Button does not receive aria-busy, so assistive technologies are not informed that the button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}> → <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy (contact form submit)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit Button in ContactForm also passes a “loading” prop rather than the expected “isLoading”, so aria-busy is not set and the loading state is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}> → <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> with onClick lacks button semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay <div className="modal-overlay" has an onClick={onClose} handler but no role="button", tabIndex, or onKeyDown handler. Keyboard‑only users cannot activate the overlay to close the dialog, violating WCAG 2.2 Keyboard accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1385: <div  
    Line 1386:   className="modal-overlay"  
    Line 1387:   role="button" // add role  
    Line 1388:   tabIndex={0} // make focusable  
    Line 1389:   aria-label="Close dialog overlay" // accessible name  
    Line 1390:   onClick={onClose}  
    Line 1391:   onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key===KEY.escape||e.key==='Enter'||e.key===' ')onClose();}}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop <div> with onClick lacks button semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" has an onClick={onClose} handler but no role="button", tabIndex, or onKeyDown handler, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1245: <div  
    Line 1246:   className="mobile-nav-backdrop"  
    Line 1247:   role="button" // add role  
    Line 1248:   tabIndex={0} // make focusable  
    Line 1249:   aria-label="Close mobile navigation" // accessible name  
    Line 1250:   onClick={onClose}  
    Line 1251:   onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key===KEY.escape||e.key==='Enter'||e.key===' ')onClose();}}

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email `<TextInput>` missing required markup
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is required but does not have the native `required` attribute nor `aria‑required="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore do not announce the field as required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit `<Button>` loading state not announced (`aria‑busy`)
issuesFound.explanation: The button uses a custom `loading` prop, but the underlying `<Button>` component only reads `isLoading`. Consequently `aria‑busy` is never set, so screen readers are not informed that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Full name” `<TextInput>` aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The input includes `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"` but no element with that ID is rendered, breaking the association between the field and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Provide an error element with matching ID or remove the attribute. Example fix: add `<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />` after the `<TextInput>` or delete the `aria-describedby` attribute.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” `<TextInput>` aria‑describedby points to non‑existent error element

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” `<Textarea>` aria‑describedby includes a non‑existent error ID

issuesFound.fix: Line 642:    <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons indicate selection only by CSS; without aria‑pressed the selected state is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 714:    <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: The link for the current page is styled with nav-link--current but does not have aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the active navigation item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340:    <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links use mobile-nav__link--current to indicate the current page but lack aria‑current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 560:    <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection <section> missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero <section> has no aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, so it provides no name to AT when navigating landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658:    <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar <section> missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar <section> lacks aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making the landmark unnamed for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735:    <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard <article> missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing plan card’s <article> is not labelled via aria‑labelledby, so AT cannot associate the card with its heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950:    <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> with onClick lacks role, tabIndex, and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div> that closes the dialog on click is not a button; it lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space, violating keyboard accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085:    <div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(ev)=>{if(ev.key==='Enter'||ev.key===' ') {ev.preventDefault(); onClose();}}>

issuesFound.title: Buttons use loading prop instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is never set
issuesFound.explanation:
The <Button> component expects an isLoading prop to set aria‑busy, but the forms pass loading={isSubmitting}. Consequently the loading state is not announced to AT.  
  Fix (NewsletterForm):    <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>  
  Fix (ContactForm):    <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: <TextInput> does not forward aria‑invalid when invalid prop is true
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop but never sets aria‑invalid, so error state is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560:    <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Field error messages are not linked via aria-describedby to their inputs
issuesFound.explanation:
Inputs set aria-describedby to IDs like newsletter-email-error or contact-name-error, but no element with those IDs is rendered, so the error text is not announced.  
  Fix (NewsletterForm):    <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} /> (rendered inside FormGroup)  
  Fix (ContactForm):    <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} /> (and similarly for email, subject, message) placed adjacent to each input.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation submenu toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands a mobile submenu has aria-controls but no aria‑expanded, so AT cannot know if the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540:    <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: <Button> component’s loadingLabel prop is not used for aria‑label when loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button’s accessible name remains the visible spinner, not the loadingLabel, which may be more descriptive for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470:    <button … aria-label={isLoading ? loadingLabel : undefined}> (add aria-label when isLoading)

'contact-name-error' : undefined} />`  
  (Apply analogous changes to the other three inputs.)

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria‑invalid when in error state (NewsletterForm email)
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<TextInput>` receives `invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}` but does not output `aria‑invalid="true"`; AT users cannot detect the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify `TextInput` component to forward `invalid` as `aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}` or add the attribute directly here:

issuesFound.title: Touch target size too small (Search button)
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments or touch device users to activate the control accurately.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (Search button icon)
issuesFound.explanation: The search button icon colour (#94a3b8) on the white background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum requirement for non-text UI components (WCAG 1.4.11). Users with low vision may not perceive the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 811: color: var(--colour-neutral-700);

issuesFound.title: Touch target size too small (Checkbox)
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum threshold (WCAG 2.5.8). This is too small for reliable activation by users with dexterity limitations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast (FAQ chevron icon)
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion chevron icon colour (#94a3b8) on the white background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing the 3:1 minimum for graphical objects (WCAG 1.4.11). The state indicator may be invisible to users with contrast sensitivity loss.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1895: color: var(--colour-neutral-700);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative (Footer navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have `text-decoration: none` and rely solely on colour change on hover to indicate interactivity (WCAG 1.4.1). Users who cannot perceive colour differences may not identify these elements as links.

issuesFound.fix: Line 951: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8). While it exceeds the 24px minimum, it may still be difficult to activate accurately on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 604: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label text uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.75) on a brand colour background (#005fcc). The effective contrast ratio is approximately 3.4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (14px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 856: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer copyright text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom paragraph text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on --colour-neutral-900 (#0f172a). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.75:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "Not included" cells
issuesFound.explanation: The table cells indicating "Not included" use --colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.3:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text (20px) and conveys status information that may be missed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 966: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on recommended plan badge
issuesFound.explanation: The badge inside the active pricing toggle button uses white text on a semi-transparent white background over a blue button. The effective contrast is approximately 2.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 903: .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge { background-color: var(--colour-success-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without sufficient non-colour differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom paragraph have text-decoration: none at rest. They are distinguished from surrounding text by color alone, but the contrast between the link color and surrounding text color is low (1.66:1), failing SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1142: .footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value in the same block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for any element not covered by more specific rules.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value in the same block. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which renders content visible on screen instead of hiding it. This exposes helper text to sighted users when it should be visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable this transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible replacement (color, shadow, or border). This hides the focus indicator for all keyboard-navigable elements that do not have a more specific override, blocking keyboard access.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible replacement. Buttons using the .btn class will have no visible focus ring for keyboard users, failing WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which renders content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only. This clutters the interface and defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 356 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active state uses a transform animation (translateY), but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override for this selector. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Deprecated ARIA attribute `aria-dropeffect` used in file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module sets the `aria-dropeffect` attribute on the upload zone element during drag events and initialization. This attribute was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and is no longer supported by modern assistive technologies, potentially causing confusion or being ignored.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3663: removeAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect');
Line 3674: removeAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect');

issuesFound.title: Play/pause button `aria-pressed` state not initialized if auto-play is disabled
issuesFound.explanation: In `testimonialsCarousel.init`, the `play()` function (which sets `aria-pressed="true"` on the play button) is only called if `autoPlay` is enabled. If auto-play is disabled, the button exists but lacks an initial `aria-pressed` state, leaving screen reader users unaware of whether the carousel is currently playing or paused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3403: if (_playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: Invalid `aria-checked` attribute applied to native button element in pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module applies `aria-checked` to the toggle element (`_toggleEl`) to indicate state. However, if the element is a native `<button>`, `aria-checked` is not a valid state (buttons support `aria-pressed`). This mismatch can confuse assistive technologies about the control's role and state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2958: if (_toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); }

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs use `aria-pressed` instead of `aria-selected`
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module treats filter tabs as a tablist but applies `aria-pressed` to the tab buttons. The correct state attribute for tabs within a tablist is `aria-selected`. Using `aria-pressed` misrepresents the widget pattern to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1775: setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher lacks keyboard activation handler for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher` module listens for click events on `[data-theme-toggle]` elements but does not attach a `keydown` handler for Enter or Space. If these elements are not native buttons (e.g., divs or spans), keyboard users cannot activate them to change the theme.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4645: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: Roving tabindex utility missing Enter/Space activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `createRovingTabindex` utility handles arrow key navigation but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate the focused item. If the roving items are not native interactive elements (like buttons or links), keyboard users can navigate to them but cannot activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); setActive(items[idx], true); break;

issuesFound.explanation: In fileUploadField._addFile, when validation fails, an error is announced via liveRegion but aria-invalid="true" is not set on the binding.inputEl. Screen readers may not associate the error with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5610: binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(`${file.name}: ${validation.error}`);

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips container updates not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle elements missing keyboard event handler
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function adds a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements. If these elements are not native buttons (e.g. divs), they lack a keydown handler for Enter/Space, blocking keyboard access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6290: document.addEventListener('click', function _onThemeClick(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; const newTheme = btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle'); if (newTheme && THEMES.includes(newTheme)) { set(newTheme); } else { toggle(); } }); document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === 'Enter' || ev.key === ' ') { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds classes and locks scroll but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the _trigger element. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that leaves screen readers unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not initialize or activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable and imports createFocusTrap, but open() never initializes or activates the trap. This allows keyboard focus to escape the mobile drawer to the underlying page content, violating focus containment requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate(); before liveRegion.announce(...);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on inst.trigger. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", causing state mismatch for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from explicitly announcing the field as invalid beyond reading the error description.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid function: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); before addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The open function announces the expansion via liveRegion.announce, but the close function does not announce the collapse. Screen reader users are not informed when an answer is collapsed, degrading the interactive experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.close function: Add liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); before emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion _onKeydown function lacks Enter/Space toggle support for non-native triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The function handles Arrow, Home, and End keys for navigation but ignores Enter and Space. Since faqRegistry.parse assigns role="button" to non-native triggers (divs), they must respond to these keys to toggle the accordion state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion._onKeydown function: Add case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break; inside the switch statement.

issuesFound.title: searchResultsHighlighter highlight function modifies DOM without announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function injects <mark> elements into the DOM via replaceChild to highlight search terms but does not announce this change. Users relying on live regions are not notified that content has been dynamically updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line: searchResultsHighlighter.highlight function: Add liveRegion.announce(`${tokens.length} term${tokens.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} highlighted in results`); after _highlightTokensInElement(container, tokens);

issuesFound.title: pricing init function does not initialize comparison table state
issuesFound.explanation: The module includes a setComparisonTableExpanded function to manage the comparison table toggle but never calls it during initialization. If the HTML contains this toggle, its aria-expanded state and visibility will not match the initial application state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing.init function: Add if (CONFIG.pricing.comparisonTableExpanded) setComparisonTableExpanded(CONFIG.pricing.comparisonTableExpanded); before debug('pricing.init: ready');

fpTitles: faqAccordion _onKeydown function lacks Enter/Space toggle support for non-native triggers

issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after _lockScroll();

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates function does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles the CSS active class on filter buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users will not be informed which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
