issuesFound.title: mobileNav module Focus Trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never assigned a createFocusTrap instance. When the mobile nav opens, focus is not contained within the drawer, allowing users to tab into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav module, open function: add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate(); before emitting the open event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not move focus to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button or previous element. Focus should be moved to the first focusable element inside the drawer for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: add const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); after _lockScroll();

issuesFound.title: pricing _updatePlanCard price updates not announced via live region

issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing._updatePlanCard function: add plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function updates the visual state of the navigation drawer and announces the change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers perceive the menu as closed even when it is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The corresponding _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving assistive technology users unaware that the dropdown is currently expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it neglects to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from accurately determining the expanded state of the accordion item.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Without this attribute, assistive technologies do not programmatically detect that the field is in an error state, relying solely on visual cues or description text.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens via _openInstance, the state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. While mobileNav and faqAccordion announce their state changes, dropdownMenu relies only on custom events, leaving users unaware that a menu has expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel and updates aria-expanded but does not announce the closure to screen readers. Users may be left wondering where the menu went if they did not trigger the close action themselves.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function injects a dialog, traps focus, and focuses the first button, but it does not announce the banner's appearance via a live region. Screen reader users may miss the context of why focus has suddenly moved.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play function does not announce autoplay start
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play function updates the button's aria-pressed state and label but does not announce that autoplay has started. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that slides will now advance automatically.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into the drawer (`_drawer`). Keyboard users remain on the trigger button behind the overlay, trapping them or causing confusion. Focus should move to the first focusable element inside the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons (`inst.trigger`) have `aria-expanded` initialized in `register`, but it is never updated when the dropdown opens or closes. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change in the same rule block. This removes the default browser focus ring for all keyboard-focusable elements, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users from seeing when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`, which keeps content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animations missing prefers-reduced-motion transition override
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple components define transitions on transform properties (btn, hero-image img, feature-card, feature-card a::after, stat-item, pricing-card) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` blocks only reset the transform value without disabling the transition itself. This can cause vestibular issues as the browser still animates the property change.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The `--colour-text-secondary` custom property is set to `#aaaaaa`. When this color is used on the default white background (`#ffffff`), it produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. This affects elements such as blockquotes (line 284), section headers (line 388), and input hints (line 1388).
issuesFound.fix: Line 102: `--colour-text-secondary: #767676;` (or any color that provides at least 4.5:1 contrast against white)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for focus ring
issuesFound.explanation: The `--colour-focus-ring` custom property is set to `#aaaaaa`. When used as a focus indicator on white backgrounds, this provides only 2.3:1 contrast, failing the 3:1 contrast requirement for focus indicators and UI components.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: `--colour-focus-ring: #005fcc;` (or any color that provides at least 3:1 contrast against white)

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation on interaction. issuesFound.title: Skip link missing reduced-motion override for transform

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition lacks reduced-motion disable

issuesFound.title: Feature card transform transition lacks reduced-motion disable

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow transform lacks reduced-motion disable

issuesFound.title: Stat item transform transition lacks reduced-motion disable

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transform transition lacks reduced-motion disable

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs text readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word spacing reduces readability and should be avoided.

issuesFound.title: Blockquote text colour contrast insufficient

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers, potentially exposing hidden text visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 388: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion animation without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, which may cause vestibular disorders for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488: .skip-link { position: fixed; top: var(--space-3); left: var(--space-3); z-index: var(--z-toast); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); border: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); transform: translateY(-200%); transition: transform var(--transition-base); white-space: nowrap; } and add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, potentially causing issues for users with vestibular disorders when buttons are activated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may trigger vestibular symptoms when the image loads or receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 863: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image hover motion without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img:hover selector applies a transform without a prefers-reduced-motion override, potentially causing vestibular issues when users hover over the image.
issuesFound.fix: Line 867: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg); box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector uses #5588bb (foreground text on dark) against #ffffff background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.6:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as underline on hover or focus, causing links to be distinguished by colour alone and failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~395: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.fix: Line ~489: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow, making content visible to sighted users while also failing to hide it properly from screen readers in some contexts. It should use absolute positioning with clipping to ensure content is available only to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~427: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform and box-shadow that creates motion, but the prefers-reduced-motion media query at line ~684 contains empty rules that do not disable this transition. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~684: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .logo-list img

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .step

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items have transitions on border-color and box-shadow that create motion when expanded/collapsed, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1508: `.faq-item { border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); overflow: hidden; transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }` and add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .form-group input, select, textarea
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs have transitions on border-color and box-shadow that create visual motion on focus, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1549: `.form-group input[type="text"], .form-group input[type="email"], .form-group select, .form-group textarea { width: 100%; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-base); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; }` and add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .form-group input, .form-group select, .form-group textarea { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word spacing can cause words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~363: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses background color #5588bb (var(--colour-brand-primary)) with white text. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.26:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (requires 4.5:1) and UI components (requires 3:1 for large text, but this is borderline for UI components).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~648: `.btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }` (using #004aaa which provides ~7:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses background color #ee9999 with white text. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.02:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for text (requires 4.5:1) and is insufficient for UI components.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~687: `.btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }` (using #991b1b which provides sufficient contrast)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global selector `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, and many link instances (navigation, footer, social links) rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive the links.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~357: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative non-color indicator such as `a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, removing the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements throughout the site.
issuesFound.fix: Line 397: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline in its focus-visible state without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the control has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: The site search submit button removes the focus outline without a visible replacement, leaving keyboard users without indication of focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links and buttons remove the focus outline without a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from tracking their current navigation position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 541: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 541: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline without a visible alternative, hiding keyboard focus state from users navigating the card grid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 577: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which filter tab currently has focus.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused billing period control.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, making it impossible to track focus when expanding or collapsing accordion sections.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a minimum height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 672: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum

issuesFound.fix: Line 685: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes have explicit dimensions of 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target below recommended
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has dimensions of 28px × 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 494: `.nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target below recommended
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a minimum height of 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size, making them potentially difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 833: `.pricing-toggle button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible overflow instead of absolute positioning and clipping, making supposedly screen-reader-only content visible to all users and breaking the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties and applies translateY transforms on :active, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 639: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images have transition and hover transforms that rotate and move the image spatially, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 696 is empty and does not disable these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Featured pricing cards have hover transforms that scale and translate the card, but the existing reduced motion media query at line 880 only targets .pricing-card:hover, not the --featured variant, leaving the animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The skip navigation link uses transform and transition to slide in from off-screen, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 457 is empty and does not disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 457: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button motion animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition properties and applies translateY on hover, but lacks a reduced motion override for this specific selector, which is more specific than the base .btn rule.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 457: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the page, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline but does not provide a replacement focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. Buttons rely on the global :focus-visible style which is also removed, leaving no visible focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Small checkboxes are difficult for users with motor impairments to accurately select.

issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility. Users with motor impairments may find this button difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target may be below recommended size

issuesFound.title: Social link touch target may be below recommended size

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only selector uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality for screen reader users and may confuse sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Button transition lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-fast) which causes spatial movement when activated. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-slower) which creates a spatial animation on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 534 is empty and does not disable this animation, which can trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow animation lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after selector includes transition: transform var(--transition-fast) which animates the arrow movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover animation lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector includes transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) which creates spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only covers .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant, leaving this animation active for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces the space between words below normal levels. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and those using magnification.

issuesFound.title: Skip link colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1 is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, making the skip link difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Primary button colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1 is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, making the button text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Danger button colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999). The contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1 is well below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, making the button text nearly invisible for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Inline code colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The code selector uses color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9). The contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1 is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, making inline code difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Required marker colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker selector uses color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) on the default white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1 is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, making the required field indicator difficult to see for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Statistics label colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background ranging from #5588bb to #bb88ee. The effective contrast is well below 4.5:1, making the statistic labels nearly invisible for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter section heading colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section h2 selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on a gradient background from #5588bb to #bb88ee. The contrast ratio against the lighter purple sections is below 4.5:1, making the heading difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter section paragraph colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background. The reduced opacity combined with the colored background results in insufficient contrast for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form label colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .form-group label selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9) on a gradient background. While closer to white, the opacity reduction may still result in insufficient contrast against portions of the gradient background.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter input hint colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .input-hint selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.65) on a gradient background. The contrast ratio is well below 4.5:1, making the hint text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally without ensuring that all links have a non-colour visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change on hover/focus. Users with colour vision deficiencies may be unable to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel does not return focus to trigger button when closed via Escape key
issuesFound.explanation: When a user presses Escape to close an expanded FAQ answer panel, the JavaScript hides the panel but does not programmatically return keyboard focus to the triggering button. This causes focus to be lost to the document body, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and creating a keyboard trap for screen reader and keyboard-only users who must navigate back to find the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add `item.trigger.focus();` immediately after calling `close(item, true);` within the KEYS.ESCAPE case block, or modify the `close` function to accept a `returnFocus` parameter that focuses the trigger when true.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons incorrectly use aria-selected attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets `aria-selected` on the billing period buttons (month/year/quarter). The `aria-selected` attribute is reserved for widgets with role `tab`, `gridcell`, or `option`. These are `<button>` elements acting as toggles, which should use `aria-pressed` to indicate their selected state. Using `aria-selected` on buttons causes screen readers to announce the state incorrectly or ignore it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1760: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and retain only `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for these toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Discount code status element dynamically adds aria-live attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code dynamically sets `aria-live="assertive"` on the status element only after an error occurs. Screen readers may fail to recognize dynamically injected aria-live regions; live regions must be present in the DOM with the attribute already set to ensure announcements are captured.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Ensure the `_statusEl` is created or selected with `aria-live="polite"` (or "assertive") already set in the initial markup or creation (around line 1840), and remove the dynamic `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'assertive');` line.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The script selects the mobile menu toggle button via `qs(SELECTORS.navToggle)` and enhances it with `aria-expanded` and `aria-controls`, but does not verify or set an accessible name (aria-label). If the underlying HTML lacks text content or an aria-label (e.g., using only a "hamburger" icon), the button will be announced as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: After selecting `_trigger`, add: `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu'); }`

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The controller selects the scroll-to-top button via `qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn)` but does not ensure it has an accessible name. If the HTML element lacks text content or an aria-label, screen reader users will not know the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: After selecting `_btn`, add: `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_btn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); }`

issuesFound.title: Site search clear button missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The script selects the clear button (`_clearBtn`) to toggle its visibility but does not ensure it has an accessible name. Clear buttons are typically icon-only and require an aria-label to be announced as "Clear search" rather than being silent or announced as "button".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: After selecting `_clearBtn`, add: `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function enhances trigger buttons with `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but does not verify that the trigger has an accessible name. Dropdown triggers often rely on icons or visual cues alone, leaving them unlabeled for assistive technology if the HTML is incomplete.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: After ensuring `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, add: `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', 'Open menu'); }`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the code ensures FAQ triggers have `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` if they aren't native buttons, it does not ensure they have an accessible name. If the HTML uses an icon or lacks text, the trigger will be inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: After setting role and tabindex, add: `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', item.question || 'Toggle answer'); }`

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (Grid/List) are selected via delegate and update `aria-pressed`, but the code does not ensure they have accessible names describing the view mode they represent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: In the `_onViewToggle` function or during init, ensure each button has an aria-label: `if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', mode === 'list' ? 'Switch to list view' : 'Switch to grid view'); }`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button missing accessible name enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel selects the play/pause button (`_playBtn`) and toggles `aria-pressed`, but does not ensure the button has an accessible name (e.g., "Pause automatic slide rotation").
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: After selecting `_playBtn`, add: `if (_playBtn && !_playBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.pauseCarousel || 'Pause automatic slide rotation'); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog (it traps focus and blocks interaction with the rest of the page) but does not have aria-modal="true". Screen readers may not announce it as a modal, causing confusion about the boundaries of the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` after selecting the drawer element in the init function.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher doesn't ensure accessible names on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher selects buttons via [data-theme-toggle] but does not verify or set aria-label attributes. If the HTML contains only icon elements without text, these buttons will have no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4880: Add `if (!btn.hasAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', 'Switch to ' + newTheme + ' theme');` within the click handler before setting the theme.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button lacks fallback accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button's aria-label depends on STRINGS.labels.clearSearch, which may be undefined. If the string is missing, the button may have no accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Change to `setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');` when configuring the clear button.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField clear method doesn't announce empty state
issuesFound.explanation: When all files are removed via the clear() method, the function removes all entries from the DOM but does not announce to screen readers that the upload queue is now empty, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4158: Add `liveRegion.announce('All files removed from upload queue');` at the end of the clear function.

issuesFound.title: pricing module doesn't announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price values update visually with animation, but the new price amounts are not announced to screen reader users. Users navigating with keyboard may not be aware that the prices have changed after toggling the billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3390: Add `liveRegion.announce('Price updated to ' + priceFormatter.format(periodPrice, plan.currency) + ' per ' + period);` within the _updatePlanCard function after updating the price display.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar lacks descriptive aria-valuetext
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar updates aria-valuenow with the percentage, but aria-valuetext only indicates the step number without the step title. Screen reader users hear only "Step 2 of 3" without knowing which step they are on.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: Change to `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', 'Step ' + (stepIndex + 1) + ' of ' + total + ': ' + _steps[stepIndex].title);` to include the step title in the announcement.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion animateHeight parameter mismatch prevents panel expansion
issuesFound.explanation: The `animateHeight` function expects parameters `(el, expanding, duration, onComplete)` where `expanding` is a boolean and `duration` is a number. In `faqAccordion.open` (line 2847) and `faqAccordion.close` (line 2872), the code passes an options object as the third argument and the string `'auto'` or number `0` as the second argument. This causes the animation to fail, leaving FAQ panels visually collapsed or expanded incorrectly and hiding content from users who do not have reduced motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: `animateHeight(item.panel, true, CONFIG.faq.animDuration || 300, null);` Line 2872: `animateHeight(item.panel, false, CONFIG.faq.animDuration || 300, () => { if (!item.open) item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); });`

issuesFound.title: Pricing animateNumber parameter mismatch prevents price updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `animateNumber` function signature is `(from, to, duration, onUpdate, easing, onComplete)`. In `pricing._updatePlanCard`, the code calls `animateNumber(plan.priceDisplay, from, periodPrice, {options})`, passing the DOM element as the first argument (expected number), the from value as second (expected number), the to value as third (expected duration), and an options object as fourth (expected function). This breaks the price animation, potentially leaving price text blank or not updated, preventing users from accessing pricing information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: `animateNumber(from, periodPrice, CONFIG.pricing.priceAnimDuration || 400, (val) => { plan.priceDisplay.textContent = priceFormatter.format(round(val, 2), plan.currency); }, 'easeOutCubic', () => { plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-label', target); });`

issuesFound.title: Carousel live region announcements during auto-play create excessive verbosity
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.goTo` function calls `liveRegion.announce` on every slide change, including during automatic rotation. Screen reader users will hear repetitive announcements ("Slide 1 of 4", "Slide 2 of 4", etc.) every few seconds while the carousel auto-plays, making it difficult to consume other content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3547: Add a parameter to distinguish manual vs automatic navigation: `if (direction !== 'auto') { liveRegion.announce(...); }` and update the auto-play timer to call `goTo(_current + 1, 'auto')`.

issuesFound.title: Search input combobox missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: In `siteSearch._fetchAndRender`, when search suggestions are loading, `srUtils.setBusy(_statusEl, true)` is called on the status element, but the search input itself (which has `role="combobox"`) does not have `aria-busy="true"` set. Assistive technologies cannot convey the loading state of the combobox to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-busy', 'true');` before the fetch call, and `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-busy', 'false');` in the try/catch finally blocks after loading completes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria-controls dependency on drawer ID
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init`, the code only sets `aria-controls` on the toggle button if `_drawer.id` exists (`if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls') && _drawer.id)`). If the HTML markup lacks an `id` attribute on the navigation drawer element, the relationship between the toggle button and the drawer is not established, breaking the disclosure pattern for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1795: Ensure the drawer has an ID before setting aria-controls: `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) { const drawerId = _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); }`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard inconsistent ARIA tab pattern
issuesFound.explanation: In `multiStepWizard.init`, step panels are assigned `role="tabpanel"` (line 4052), but the navigation controls are `<button>` elements without `role="tab"`. This creates an inconsistent tab interface that does not follow the WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern, potentially confusing screen reader users who expect tab-specific keyboard navigation (arrow keys between tabs).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4052: Remove `role="tabpanel"` from step panels: `// step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` Alternatively, if implementing a tab pattern, add `role="tab"` to the prev/next buttons and manage tab activation appropriately.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-describedby for file constraints
issuesFound.explanation: In `fileUploadField.bind`, the upload zone receives an `aria-label` but does not reference the file constraints (max size, allowed types) via `aria-describedby`. Users may not be aware of restrictions until they attempt to upload invalid files.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320: Create a description element with the constraints text and reference it: `const descId = srUtils.ensureId(createElement('div', {class:'sr-only'}, `Max ${config.maxFileSizeMB}MB, types: ${config.allowedMimeTypes.join(', ')}`), 'upload-desc'); zoneEl.appendChild(descId); setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', descId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, though close() sets it to "false". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1815: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap or move focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the code initializes a focus trap variable `_trap` but never activates it, and focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or first focusable element. Keyboard users cannot access the opened menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820: Add `_trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce this change via the liveRegion announcer. Screen reader users may not be aware that content has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view but does not announce this change. Users may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance before or after the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._update does not announce button visibility changes
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button appears and disappears based on scroll position (a dynamic content change), but this status change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrollToTopVisible || 'Scroll to top button visible');` when `shouldShow && !_visible`, and `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrollToTopHidden || 'Scroll to top button hidden');` in the else branch.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher.init does not initialize aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher buttons use aria-pressed to indicate the active theme, but this attribute is only set when a button is clicked, not when the module initializes. On page load, screen readers do not know which theme is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3135: After determining `_current`, add code to set initial aria-pressed: `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { const btnTheme = btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === _current)); });`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle lacks required ARIA roles
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons use aria-selected (appropriate for tabs) but the JavaScript does not ensure the container has role="tablist" or that the buttons have role="tab". Screen readers will not recognize this as a tab interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: Add `periodContainer.setAttribute('role', 'tablist');` and inside _syncToggleState or init, add `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab'));` to ensure proper semantics.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles CSS classes and locks scroll but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation drawer because the attribute remains stale (false or undefined) while the drawer is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel opens, the function adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The paired _closeInstance function sets it to "false", creating an asymmetric state where the trigger always reports collapsed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ item panel and updates internal state, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", so screen readers only hear the collapsed state and never the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function after setting `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, and the valid state removal in markValid removes the attribute, creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1175: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes the visible UI significantly, but neither function calls liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed. Users may not realize the menu is available or that it has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` inside _openInstance. Line 1780: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop uses undefined selector constant
issuesFound.explanation: The code references `SELECTORS.skipLink` (singular), but the SELECTORS object defines `skipLinks` (plural). This causes `qs(SELECTORS.skipLink)` to return null, preventing focus from returning to the skip link and falling back to document.body, which disorients keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1887: Change `const skipLink = qs(SELECTORS.skipLink);` to `const skipLink = qs(SELECTORS.skipLinks);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving into the newly visible drawer. Additionally, the code declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap or activates it. Keyboard users cannot easily access the navigation links without manually tabbing forward.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: Add focus management: `const firstLink = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstLink.focus({ preventScroll: true });` and initialize the trap: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update

This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state, even though the close() function at line 2220 sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2193: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of the input to users. The paired markValid() function at line 1589 correctly removes aria-invalid, but since markInvalid never sets it, the attribute is never present.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1579: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid="false" (complementary to Issue 5)
issuesFound.explanation: While markValid correctly removes the aria-invalid attribute, for consistency with ARIA practices and to explicitly communicate the valid state to assistive technologies, it should set aria-invalid="false" rather than removing the attribute entirely. This ensures the field explicitly reports as valid after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1590: Change `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` to `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`
