issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by manipulating CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). This prevents screen readers from announcing whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2630: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after _open = true; in the open() function
  Line 2645: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); immediately after _open = false; in the close() function

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels by removing/adding the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but they fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger elements (inst.trigger). This leaves the accessibility tree out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2763: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true; in _openInstance
  Line 2775: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); immediately after inst.open = false; in _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions animate the FAQ answer panels open and closed but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons (item.trigger) to reflect the current expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4158: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inside the open() function after item.open = true;
  Line 4172: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); inside the close() function after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links error messages to fields using aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to programmatically identify fields that have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1926: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); immediately after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes error descriptions from aria-describedby but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid states on fields that have been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1944: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); inside the markValid function after updating aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the function updates the visible product cards, count badges, and empty states, but does not announce the result count or state changes to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); immediately after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); in applyFilters()

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price displays update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers to inform users that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3585: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`); immediately after _updateSavingsMessage(period); in setPeriod()

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function discovers the trigger and drawer elements but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the page loads, leaving the initial collapsed state ambiguous for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); immediately after _initialised = true; in the init function (after verifying _trigger exists)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code does not instantiate or activate a focus trap. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page content, violating focus order and making the drawer difficult to use.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but the JavaScript only toggles CSS classes. It does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the active state, so screen readers cannot identify which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _syncTabAriaStates, after toggleClass): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. When validation passes, markValid removes the describedby link but fails to clear aria-invalid to false. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
    Line (in markValid, after the if block): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: characterCounter does not associate counter with input field
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter element is inserted into the DOM but is not linked to the input field via aria-describedby. Screen reader users are not informed of the character count when the input receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in attach, after creating counterEl): `srUtils.addDescribedBy(field, counterEl);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter results changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates (showing/hiding cards and updating the count badge), the changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in applyFilters, after _updateEmptyState(totalMatches);):
```
    liveRegion.announce(
      totalMatches === 0 
        ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters.' 
        : (STRINGS.filterResults || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches))
    );
```

issuesFound.title: pricing module does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annually), the price values update visually but are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed of the new prices after changing the billing period.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips remove button does not manage focus after removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is removed by clicking its remove button, the chip element is removed from the DOM without moving focus to another element. Focus is lost to the document root, forcing keyboard users to start tabbing from the beginning of the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _onChipClick, at the start of the function):
```
    const nextFocus = btn.closest('.filter-chip').nextElementSibling || btn.closest('.filter-chip').previousElementSibling || _containerEl;
```
    Line (at the end of the function, after render()): `if (nextFocus) nextFocus.focus();`

fpTitles: characterCounter does not associate counter with input field
activeFilterChips remove button does not manage focus after removal

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the navigation drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1630: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after _open = true;
  Line 1652: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); immediately after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button even though the drawer is closed by default. This creates a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after _initialised = true;

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer mount does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search combobox input is created with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load. Since the suggestion panel is hidden by default, screen readers may not correctly identify the collapsed state of the combobox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2852: Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1242: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as valid, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field may remain marked as invalid to screen readers even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: Add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); after removing the error reference from aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce dynamic content changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates (showing/hiding cards and updating the count badge), the change is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology will not know that the content has updated or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3558: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); inside the filterAnimation.run callback, before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annually), the price values on the page update visually and animate, but the new prices and billing period are not announced to screen readers. This is a dynamic content change that requires a status message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4035: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); after updating the plan cards and savings message.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation does not set aria-invalid on input
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is announced via liveRegion, but the aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true" on the input field. When validation succeeds, aria-invalid is not cleared. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field's validation state when navigating the form.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4240 (inside else block for invalid): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line 4236 (inside if block for valid): Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which dropdowns are currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1470 (inside _openInstance, after inst.open = true;): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1485 (inside _closeInstance, after inst.open = false;): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically identify that the field has failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850 (inside markInvalid, after addDescribedBy call): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to "false"), leaving screen readers to believe the field is still invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3870 (inside markValid, after removing describedby refs): removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function dynamically updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter results to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filters or that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150 (inside filterAnimation.run callback, before emit): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400 (inside setPeriod, after updating plan cards): liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on triggers but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for closed dropdowns on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510 (inside register function, after setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);): setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Additionally, the init function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" when the menu starts closed. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1844 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`
  Line 1864 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`
  Line 1894 (in init function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes dropdown panels but fails to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons in the open and close functions. The register function also does not initialize aria-expanded="false" for closed dropdowns. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2074 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2089 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`
  Line 2180 (in register): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` before `_instances.push(inst);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the mismatch means screen readers will incorrectly announce the expanded state when an item is opened, as the attribute remains false or undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3524 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module implements a combobox pattern where the search input controls a suggestion listbox, but it never sets aria-expanded on the input element to indicate whether the suggestions are visible. The init function also fails to initialize aria-expanded="false", violating the required state management for combobox patterns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2840 (in _open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 2850 (in _close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`
  Line 2916 (in init function after mounting listbox): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module assigns role="switch" to the billing period toggle element but the setPeriod function does not update the aria-checked attribute to reflect the current state (e.g., true for annual, false for monthly). The init function also does not initialize aria-checked, leaving screen readers unaware of the switch state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3245 (in setPeriod function): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year');` after `_syncToggleState(period);`
  Line 3360 (in init function): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', _pricingState.period === 'year');` after event bindings are attached.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tabs act as toggle buttons where one is active at a time, but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes and does not update aria-pressed to indicate which tab is currently pressed or active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100 (in _syncTabAriaStates): Within the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid utility function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from programmatically identifying the field as having a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1124 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid utility function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to "false"), leaving screen readers to believe the field is still invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136 (in markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the count badge to show the number of filtered products, but the badge element is not a live region and the function does not use a live region announcement, so screen reader users are not notified when the product count changes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3112 (in _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` at the end of the function after updating the badge text.

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price values update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen readers about the new prices, leaving users unaware of the price change unless they manually navigate to the price element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3205 (in _updatePlanCard): After setting `plan.priceDisplay.textContent = target;`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} for ${plan.id} plan`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the menu starts in the closed state, violating the requirement that aria-expanded be present and correctly reflect the initial state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1894 (in init function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;` and the element existence checks.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets the panel to hidden but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, leaving screen readers unaware of the collapsible state until the first user interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180 (in register function): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` and before `_instances.push(inst);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function creates a combobox pattern but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the search input before the suggestions are shown, failing to indicate the initial collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2916 (in init function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', false);` after the listbox is mounted and before event bindings are attached.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop module attaches a click listener to the button element but does not verify it is a native button or add role="button", tabindex, and keyboard handlers. If the HTML uses a div, it will be unreachable by keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Skip link target ID mismatch for search
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link references "#site-search" but the SearchInput component in SiteHeader is rendered with `id="header-search"`, causing the skip link to target a non-existent element. Keyboard users attempting to skip to search will find focus does not move to the search field.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `href="#site-search"` to `href="#header-search"` in the SkipLinks component.

issuesFound.title: Skip link target missing on mobile viewports
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link references "#main-nav" which only exists on the DesktopNav component that is conditionally rendered when `!isMobile`. On mobile viewports, the element with id="main-nav" is removed from the DOM, breaking the skip link for mobile keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (DesktopNav): Change `<nav id="main-nav"` to ensure the element is always rendered in the DOM (e.g., by moving the id to a container that persists across breakpoints) or remove the skip link when the target is absent. Alternatively, add `id="main-nav"` to the mobile navigation container in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error prop prevents error message display in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects the prop `errorMessage` to render error text, but NewsletterForm passes `error`. This prevents validation errors from being rendered and announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of form validation failures.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` in the NewsletterForm FormGroup usage.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error prop prevents error display for Full name field
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` prop, but ContactForm passes `error`. This prevents the Full name field's validation errors from being rendered or announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` in the ContactForm Full name FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error prop prevents error display for Email field
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` prop, but ContactForm passes `error`. This prevents the Email field's validation errors from being rendered or announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` in the ContactForm Email FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error prop prevents error display for Subject field
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` prop, but ContactForm passes `error`. This prevents the Subject field's validation errors from being rendered or announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}` in the ContactForm Subject FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error prop prevents error display for Message field
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` prop, but ContactForm passes `error`. This prevents the Message field's validation errors from being rendered or announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}` in the ContactForm Message FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop in NewsletterForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop to render content, but NewsletterForm passes the error text as children. The component destructures only `id` and `message` from props and ignores children, causing the server error message to not be rendered or announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert">Something went wrong...</ErrorMessage>` to `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />`.

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop in ContactForm
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop, but ContactForm passes the error text as children. The component ignores children, so the submission error message is not displayed or announced to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" role="alert">Submission failed...</ErrorMessage>` to `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" role="alert" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />`.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly holds aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog element. This causes assistive technologies to incorrectly identify the modal boundary, potentially failing to constrain focus within the dialog or announce it properly as a modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1032: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>` Line 1033: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button references non-existent element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The submit Button has aria-describedby="newsletter-status", but the element with this id only exists when status is 'error' or 'success'. When the form is idle or submitting, screen readers encounter a broken reference, causing confusion about the button's description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form removes focused elements from DOM on success without focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter form submits successfully, the entire form element is replaced with a success paragraph. If focus was on the submit button, it is lost to the document body. The success message is not programmatically focused, leaving screen reader users without context of the status change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 918: `{status === 'success' ? (` Line 919: `

issuesFound.title: Contact form removes focused elements from DOM on success without focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the contact form submits successfully, the form is removed from the DOM and replaced with a success message. If focus was on the submit button or any form field, focus is lost to the document body. The success message does not receive programmatic focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 998: `{status === 'success' ? (` Line 999: `

` Line 1000: `✅ Message received! We'll be in touch soon.` Line 1001: `

` And add `const successRef = useRef(null);` with `useEffect(() => { if (status === 'success') successRef.current?.focus(); }, [status]);`

issuesFound.title: Notification dismissal removes focused element from DOM causing focus loss
issuesFound.explanation: When the last notification is dismissed, NotificationContainer returns null and removes the entire container from the DOM. If keyboard focus was on the dismiss button of that notification, focus is lost to the document body.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1072: `if (notifications.length === 0) return <div aria-live="off" style={{position:'absolute',width:0,height:0,overflow:'hidden'}} />;` Or alternatively, move focus to the trigger element before calling onDismiss.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId and incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed the prop `error` instead of `errorMessage`, so the error text is never rendered. Additionally, the `errorId` prop is omitted, so the ErrorMessage element (and its id) is never created, causing the TextInput's aria-describedby to reference a non-existent ID. Screen reader users will not hear error announcements.
issuesFound.fix: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup for full name missing errorId and incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup receives `error` instead of `errorMessage`, preventing the error from rendering, and lacks `errorId`, breaking the aria-describedby reference on the TextInput. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the validation error.
issuesFound.fix: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-name-error">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup for email missing errorId and incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup is configured with `error` rather than `errorMessage`, suppressing the error display, and omits `errorId`, leaving the TextInput's aria-describedby pointing to a missing element. Screen readers will not announce the email validation error.
issuesFound.fix: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-email-error">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup for subject missing errorId and incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: Using `error` instead of `errorMessage` prevents the error message from appearing, and the missing `errorId` causes a broken aria-describedby reference on the TextInput. Users relying on screen readers will not be informed of validation failures.
issuesFound.fix: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-subject-error">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup for message missing errorId and incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup uses the incorrect prop name `error` instead of `errorMessage`, hiding the error text, and does not provide `errorId`, resulting in an invalid aria-describedby reference on the Textarea. Assistive technology users will not receive feedback about the message field errors.
issuesFound.fix: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-message-error">

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute on the underlying `<input>`. Screen readers cannot convey the error state to users when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure trigger button controls a submenu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. The state is managed by the isOpen prop but not exposed via ARIA.
issuesFound.fix: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button expands and collapses submenu sections but does not have aria-expanded to indicate the state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" aria-expanded={isExpanded} aria-controls={sectionId} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen readers from announcing whether the answer panel is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but ThemeToggle renders it without one, leaving the button without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name

issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button is an icon-only control but lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the error state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The invalid prop is used only for styling but not exposed via aria-invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The invalid prop controls styling but is not mapped to the aria-invalid attribute, hiding the error state from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs active state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons show active state via CSS class but lack aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>
